title: Grammar · German C1
scope: C1 is mastery of the elevated, written register: extended participle constructions, alternation between nominal and verbal style (Funktionsverbgefüge), advanced reported speech, modal particles, high-register connectors, genitive prepositions and subtle conditional structures. Boundary: you produce formal, dense, nuanced text; C2 then adds only near-native stylistic finesse.

blocks.text: They condense a relative clause into a participle attribute placed before the noun. Very common in formal and technical texts.

blocks.label: Participle attribute
blocks.text: das von der Firma entwickelte Produkt

blocks.label: Equivalent relative clause
blocks.text: das Produkt, das die Firma entwickelt hat
Partizip I = active and simultaneous meaning (die steigenden Preise); Partizip II = passive/completed meaning (die gestiegenen Preise).
Nominal style concentrates information in nouns; verbal style in verbs. Switching between them is a key C1 skill.

rows: Nachdem man das Gesetz geändert hatte, …

blocks.text: Fixed verb + noun combinations that work as a unit and give a formal tone. The meaning lies in the noun.

rows: Er stellt die Methode in Frage.
Wir ziehen alle Optionen in Betracht.
Ich nehme Bezug auf Ihr Schreiben.

blocks.text: In the elevated register, reported speech combines Konjunktiv I and, when it coincides with the indicative, Konjunktiv II. It also distinguishes simultaneity from anteriority.

rows: Er sagte, er sei gekommen / er habe es getan.

blocks.text: Small words that add the speaker's tone and attitude; they are not translated literally.

rows: Wir gehen, zumal es spät ist.
Es war schwer; gleichwohl gelang es.
Es war neblig, sodass wir langsam fuhren.

blocks.text: Genitive prepositions typical of administrative and formal language.

rows: Wir kommen, es sei denn, es regnet.
Selbst wenn es schwer ist, versuche ich es.
Er ging, ohne sich zu verabschieden.

blocks.text: In long sentences, the Satzklammer (conjugated verb in position 2 + verbal part at the end) structures the whole sentence. Complements follow the te-ka-mo-lo order (temporal, causal, modal, local), with variation for emphasis.
Ich habe gestern wegen des Staus mit dem Fahrrad zur Arbeit fahren müssen.
